title: Palindromes

glossary.definition: The exact middle point around which both sides are mirror images of each other.
The individual steps of processing power a computer uses to perform a task.
Symbols like punctuation or spaces that are not letters or numbers.

hook.scenario: You need to verify if a DNA sequence is a perfect mirror image of itself.
hook.question: Do you reverse the entire sequence and check for equality, or is there a way to halt instantly at the first sign of mismatch?

why_it_exists.problem: A palindrome reads identically forward and backward. The naive approach—duplicating the string, reversing it, and comparing—requires O(N) auxiliary space and always scans the entire string even if it fails immediately.
why_it_exists.better_idea: Use the two-pointer technique to check symmetry from the outside in. If any character mismatches, halt immediately. This uses O(1) space and optimizes the average-case runtime.

mental_model.intuition: Imagine folding a piece of paper in half. For the text to be a palindrome, every letter on the left side must perfectly overlap with the corresponding letter on the right side.
mental_model.description: Validating a palindrome uses converging two-pointers checking for equality. Finding the longest palindromic substring requires the inverse: placing pointers at a 'center' and expanding outward until symmetry breaks.
mental_model.repeated_decision: During validation: `if string[left] != string[right]`, it's not a palindrome. During expansion: `if string[left] == string[right]`, expand the bounds.

invariants.inv-bounds: Everything outside the [lo, hi] window has already been confirmed to mirror.
invariants.inv-match: A palindrome reads the same both ways, so arr[lo] must equal arr[hi] at every step.
invariants.inv-palindrome: If every outer pair matches until the pointers cross, the whole string is a palindrome.

explanation:
To validate if a string is a palindrome, place a pointer at index 0 and a pointer at N-1. If they match, move inward. If they ever mismatch, return false instantly. If they cross, return true.

A more complex problem is finding the *longest* palindromic substring within a larger text. You cannot easily use outside-in pointers here. Instead, you use the 'Expand Around Center' algorithm.

A palindrome must have a center of symmetry. It's either an odd-length center (a single character, like 'c' in "racecar") or an even-length center (the imaginary gap between two identical characters, like the gap between the 'b's in "abba").

The algorithm iterates through every character in the string, treating it as a potential center. It initializes pointers at the center and expands outward. `left--` and `right++`. As long as the characters match and are within array bounds, the palindrome grows. It checks both odd and even centers for every index, keeping track of the absolute maximum length found.

key_points.title: Validation: O(N) Time
key_points.detail: Checking if a string is a palindrome takes O(N) time and O(1) space.

key_points.title: Search: O(N^2) Time
key_points.detail: Expanding around 2N-1 potential centers (N odd, N-1 even) takes O(N^2) time.

key_points.title: Early Exit
key_points.detail: Both algorithms halt the exact moment symmetry is broken, saving massive computational cycles on invalid strings.

pattern.name: Symmetrical Expansion / Convergence
recognition_cues: The problem asks for symmetrical patterns or mirror images.

failure_signals: You are using `[::-1]` in Python to reverse strings for validation.
When expanding from the center, you forget to check for the even-length (gap) centers.

engineering_examples.title: Bioinformatics
engineering_examples.problem: Identifying gene expression markers in DNA.
engineering_examples.why_this_algorithm: Restriction enzymes search for palindromic DNA sequences to cut DNA for genetic cloning. Algorithms mimicking this use palindromic expansion.

engineering_examples.title: Network Data Integrity
engineering_examples.problem: Verifying lossless transmission of symmetrical packets.
engineering_examples.why_this_algorithm: Symmetry checks validate that the payload structure wasn't corrupted in transit.

when_not_to_use.scenario: You need the absolute optimal runtime for searching massive strings
when_not_to_use.reason: Expand Around Center takes O(N^2). For truly massive datasets, Manacher's Algorithm can find the longest palindromic substring in O(N) time, though it is exceedingly complex to implement.

common_mistakes.title: Failing to sanitize input
common_mistakes.explanation: Standard palindromes ignore spaces, punctuation, and capitalization. You must skip these non-alphanumeric characters by advancing the pointers inside your while loop.

common_mistakes.title: Out of bounds on expansion
common_mistakes.explanation: When expanding from the center, the loop must always check `left >= 0` and `right < length` BEFORE checking if the characters match, or it will throw an error.

recall_questions.q: Why is two-pointer validation better than reversing the entire string?
recall_questions.answer: It uses O(1) auxiliary space instead of O(N), and can exit instantly upon the first mismatch instead of always processing the entire string.

recall_questions.q: What are the two types of palindromic centers?
recall_questions.answer: Odd-length centers (a single character) and even-length centers (the gap between two identical characters).

recall_questions.q: What is the time complexity of the 'Expand Around Center' algorithm?
recall_questions.answer: O(N^2), because it potentially expands outward up to N/2 times for each of the 2N-1 centers.

oa_questions.question: Verify if a phrase is a palindrome, ignoring non-alphanumeric characters and casing.
oa_questions.answer: Use two converging pointers. If a pointer sits on a non-alphanumeric character, move it inward until it hits a valid character. Compare the lowercase versions. If they differ, return false.

oa_questions.question: Count all palindromic substrings in a given string.
oa_questions.answer: Use the Expand Around Center approach. For each of the 2N-1 centers, expand outwards. Increment a count for each valid expansion.

practice.title: Valid Palindrome
practice.why: The ultimate test of in-place converging validation.

practice.title: Longest Palindromic Substring
practice.why: Forces you to implement the Expand Around Center logic for both odd and even lengths.

interesting_facts: Manacher's algorithm uses dynamic programming to reuse previously expanded palindrome boundaries, achieving O(N) time complexity for the longest palindrome problem.
